desc: assess ways in which animals and plants depend on each other and on their environment to meet their basic needs
describe the basic needs of plants and animals, including air, water, food, shelter, and space
identify the different ways in which plants and animals adapt to changes in their environments
describe how plants and animals go through a cycle of life, including the processes of growth, reproduction, and death
assess the impact of human activities on the use of materials that have different properties
describe and compare the properties of materials (e.g., hard/soft, rough/smooth, heavy/light, transparent/opaque)
identify objects that are made from one or more materials, and explain why specific materials are used for specific purposes
assess the environmental impact of producing and disposing of structures built by humans
identify structures in the natural environment and the built environment
describe the function of various structures and identify the materials used to build them
assess ways in which human activities affect seasonal changes and the living things that depend on seasonal patterns
describe changes in the sky and weather from season to season
identify the four seasons and describe how each season affects plants, animals, and human activities
assess the impact of human activities on growth and changes in plants
describe the basic needs of plants and demonstrate understanding of how plants meet those needs
identify the parts of a plant (root, stem, leaf, flower, fruit, seed) and describe their functions
describe the life cycle of a plant and explain how plants reproduce
assess how changes in the properties of materials can be used to benefit society
identify and describe reversible changes (e.g., bending, freezing, melting) and non-reversible changes (e.g., burning, cooking) in materials
describe the three states of matter (solid, liquid, gas) and give examples of each
assess the environmental impact of human structures and technologies on wildlife
identify structures that are built to carry a load and explain the forces acting on them
identify the properties of materials used to build strong and stable structures
assess the impact of human activities on air and water quality
describe properties of air and water and explain why they are important to living things
describe the water cycle, including evaporation, condensation, and precipitation
assess ways in which humans and other living things depend on natural habitats and communities
identify and describe different types of habitats and the living things that depend on each
classify living things in a local habitat as producers, consumers, or decomposers
demonstrate understanding of food chains as a model of energy flow between producers and consumers
assess environmental impacts of the use of materials with specific properties
compare the properties of liquids and solids and identify examples of each
identify materials that are magnetic and explain how magnets can be used to separate materials
assess the impact of strong and stable structures on society and the environment
identify structures that serve the same function and compare their designs
demonstrate understanding of what makes structures strong and stable
assess the impact of human activities on soils
describe the properties and components of soil and explain why soil is important to plants
classify different types of soils (e.g., sand, clay, loam) based on their properties
assess the impact of human activities on habitats and communities of plants and animals
demonstrate understanding of the concepts of habitat and community
describe ways in which plants and animals adapt to their environment
classify plants and animals according to their roles in a food chain or food web
assess how understanding of the properties of rocks and minerals is used in society
compare the physical properties of rocks and minerals (e.g., colour, lustre, hardness, texture)
describe how rocks are formed (igneous, sedimentary, metamorphic) and how they change over time
assess the impact of pulleys and gears on society and the environment
demonstrate understanding of how pulleys and gears act as simple machines to change force and speed
identify examples of pulleys and gears in everyday devices and explain their function
assess the impact of human activities on the natural environment
identify and describe properties of air, water, and soil and explain their importance to living things
describe the water cycle and explain how the sun drives the water cycle
assess the impact of human activities on biodiversity and the sustainability of ecosystems
demonstrate understanding of biodiversity as the variety of life on Earth
classify organisms using the hierarchical classification system (kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, species)
describe the interdependence of organisms in an ecosystem and the impact of changes on biodiversity
assess the impact on society and the environment of technologies that use the properties of matter
demonstrate understanding of the particle theory of matter to explain the properties of solids, liquids, and gases
describe and explain physical changes (reversible) and chemical changes (irreversible) in matter
assess the impact of simple machines on society and the environment
identify and describe the six types of simple machines: lever, wheel and axle, pulley, inclined plane, wedge, screw
explain how simple machines make work easier by changing the direction or size of a force
identify simple machines in compound machines and explain how they work together
assess the impact of human activities on the conservation of energy and resources
identify a variety of forms of energy, and describe how each form is used in everyday life
demonstrate understanding of the law of conservation of energy
describe how energy is stored as potential energy and transformed in a variety of everyday situations
assess the impact of human activities and technologies on biodiversity and on the sustainability of ecosystems
describe the complementary processes of photosynthesis and cellular respiration
identify the organelles in a plant cell and an animal cell and describe their functions
explain how biodiversity contributes to the sustainability of ecosystems
assess the social and environmental impact of electrical energy production and consumption
describe the properties of static and current electricity
build and describe a simple series circuit and a parallel circuit
explain how electrical energy can be transformed into other forms of energy
assess the impact of flight technology on society and the environment
explain the four forces of flight: lift, weight, thrust, and drag
describe the Bernoulli principle and explain how it relates to flight
assess the impact of space exploration and technology on society and the environment
describe the composition of the solar system and identify the characteristics of the planets
explain the effects of the relative positions and motions of Earth, the Moon, and the Sun
assess the impact of human activities on interactions within ecosystems
demonstrate understanding of the interactions between biotic and abiotic components of ecosystems
describe the processes of photosynthesis and cellular respiration and explain their role in ecosystems
explain how matter and energy flow through ecosystems using food webs
assess the social and environmental impact of the use of pure substances and mixtures
identify physical and chemical properties of pure substances and mixtures
distinguish between pure substances (elements and compounds) and mixtures (mechanical and solutions)
describe separation techniques for mixtures based on physical properties
assess the impact of structures and mechanisms on society and the environment
explain how forces and structure relate to achieve function in mechanical systems
identify and describe the mechanical advantage of gears, pulleys, and levers in compound machines
assess the impact of human activities on the quality and sustainability of water systems
describe the properties and distribution of water on Earth's surface and in the atmosphere
explain the role of the water cycle in the distribution of water on Earth
describe factors that affect the water quality of local water systems
assess the impact of scientific discoveries and technologies on human cells and body systems
identify the major organelles in animal cells and describe their functions
describe the structure and function of the main human body systems and explain how they work together
explain how cells, tissues, organs, and organ systems are organized in the human body
assess the social and environmental impact of the use of fluids
describe the properties of fluids, including viscosity and density
explain Pascal's law and describe how it applies to hydraulic and pneumatic systems
explain Archimedes' principle and describe how it applies to flotation
assess the impact of space technology on society and the environment
describe optical devices and explain how they use properties of light
explain the wave properties of light including reflection, refraction, and dispersion
assess the impact of human activities on Earth's climate systems
describe Earth's four major systems (geosphere, hydrosphere, atmosphere, biosphere) and their interactions
explain factors that affect climate and describe the causes and effects of climate change
identify ways to reduce the impact of climate change on Earth's systems
